title: Shape-constrained monotone specialist for tyre-age and finish-window logic
summary: XGBoost monotonic constraints can improve performance when domain priors are strong.
rationale: XGBoost monotonic constraints can improve performance when domain priors are strong. Here, some effects should mostly move one way within regime: higher latent wear, larger positive lap-time loss, or greater infeasibility-to-finish should generally not decrease stop risk. A constrained model can act as a lower-variance specialist that complements freer tree models.
implementation_hint: Train a second booster or EBM with monotone constraints on a compact feature set such as `TyreLife`, filtered wear state, positive `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `TyreLife + LapsRemaining - expected_compound_life`. Blend only through OOF ranks/probabilities with the unconstrained baseline.
expected_effect: Regularizes brittle threshold behavior and can improve LB robustness by adding a smoother physics-consistent vote.
risk: Wrong monotone assumptions, especially around overcut windows or wet compounds, can flatten useful local reversals.
